entity_extraction_template: Each 'Record:' line introduces one HealthcareResource entity (e.g. '注册医生数目(1)'). Each observation sentence like '注册医生数目(1) observed healthcare_rate_year2024=2.2 per 1,000 population.' contains one StatValue entity named 'healthcare_rate_year2024=2.2_per_1000'. Extract BOTH the HealthcareResource entity AND the StatValue entity from each observation line.
relation_extraction_template: For each observation line 'ENTITY observed healthcare_rate_yearYYYY=VALUE per 1,000 population.', create: (HealthcareResource: ENTITY) -[HAS_VALUE]-> (StatValue: healthcare_rate_yearYYYY=VALUE_per_1000). The target entity name MUST include both year and value (e.g. 'healthcare_rate_year2024=2.2_per_1000'). NEVER use bare numeric values like '2.2' as the target entity name.
extraction_constraints: IMPORTANT: Create a HAS_VALUE edge for EVERY observation line in the chunk.
The target entity of HAS_VALUE MUST be a composite string containing year and value (e.g. 'healthcare_rate_year2024=2.2_per_1000'), NOT a bare number.
The source entity is always the HealthcareResource name from the 'Record:' line.
Include the year in the edge keywords attribute.
Do NOT skip any observation line — each line must produce exactly one HAS_VALUE edge.
